title: astragalus regestus
intro: Astragalus regestus, also known as milk vetch or bean trefoil, is a type of perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of China and other parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Astragalus regestus include improving immune function, reducing fatigue and stress, and supporting cardiovascular health, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. Astragalus regestus can be prepared as a decoction, powder, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance its effects.
